title: 13 Best Herbal Teas For Ringworm
intro: Herbal teas for Ringworm are a natural remedy used to treat fungal infections of the skin, typically caused by Trichophyton species. These teas work by utilizing the antimicrobial properties of various herbs to combat the fungal infection, reducing inflammation, and promoting healing. Examples of herbal teas that can be used to treat ringworm include Sage tea, which has antibacterial properties that help combat the infection; Thyme tea, which contains compounds that inhibit fungal growth; Oregano tea, which has antifungal properties that help reduce inflammation; and Neem tea, which has antiseptic and antifungal properties that help promote healing. Additionally, Turmeric tea, Cinnamon tea, and Ginger tea can also be used to treat ringworm due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that help reduce symptoms and promote healing.

preparations.herb_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia teas, also known as tea tree oil tea, have been used to treat ringworm due to their antifungal and antibacterial properties. The terpenes, particularly cineole, and other bioactive constituents present in this herbal preparation help to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause ringworm. By reducing the fungal and bacterial load on the affected area, Melaleuca alternifolia teas promote wound healing, alleviate itching and inflammation, and ultimately treat ringworm. The benefits of using this herbal preparation include its natural origin, low risk of side effects, and effectiveness in treating ringworm without the need for antibiotics or steroids.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of boiling water, 1 tablespoon of dried Melaleuca alternifolia leaves, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Steep the dried leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup using the tea infuser or strainer.
Add honey to taste, if desired, and mix well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks to help treat ringworm.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Melaleuca alternifolia teas can be used to treat ringworm, but they may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. When using Melaleuca alternifolia teas to treat ringworm, it is essential to take precautions such as monitoring your skin for any signs of allergic reactions and adjusting the dosage to avoid over-exposure, as well as drinking plenty of water to prevent dehydration.
preparation_usage: Soak affected area in warm bath with 1 cup of Melaleuca alternifolia tea.
Apply a solution of 1 tablespoon of Melaleuca alternifolia oil in 1 cup of water to affected area.
Use a cotton swab to apply solution to affected area 2-3 times daily.
Repeat treatment for 2-3 weeks or until ringworm symptoms subside.
Consume 1 cup of Melaleuca alternifolia tea 2-3 times daily for internal support.

preparations.herb_desc: Eucalyptus globulus teas have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, a fungal infection that affects the skin. The antimicrobial and antifungal properties of this herbal preparation help to combat the infection, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The bioactive constituents, including eucalyptol and flavonoids, exhibit strong antifungal activity, inhibiting the growth of the fungal pathogen and alleviating symptoms. Regular consumption of Eucalyptus globulus teas has been found to be beneficial in treating ringworm, due to its ability to enhance the body's immune response and provide relief from itching and discomfort.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried Eucalyptus globulus leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and discard the leaves.
Add 1 teaspoon of honey to the tea to make it sweeter.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, preferably after meals, for 2-3 weeks.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Eucalyptus globulus teas can be used to treat ringworm, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and stomach upset in some individuals. Precautions should be taken to avoid over-consumption, as excessive use can lead to dizziness, headaches, and interact with other medications, and it's also essential to clean and dry the affected area thoroughly to prevent further infection.
preparation_usage: Soak a cotton ball in Eucalyptus globulus tea and apply directly to ringworm
Use 1-2 tablespoons of Eucalyptus globulus tea 3 times a day
Soak affected area in warm water with 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea for 30 minutes
Drink 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus tea daily to boost immune system
Continue treatment for 2-3 weeks to clear ringworm infection

preparations.herb_desc: Calendula officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, an infectious fungal skin condition, due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, including triterpenoids and flavonoids, help to inhibit the growth of fungi and reduce inflammation, making them an effective treatment for ringworm. By promoting wound healing and reducing the severity of symptoms, Calendula officinalis teas provide relief from the discomfort and itching associated with ringworm. The benefits of using these teas to treat ringworm include accelerated healing, reduced risk of infection, and minimal side effects, making them a popular natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Calendula officinalis flowers and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Calendula flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the liquid, if desired, for sweetness and soothing properties.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Calendula tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe and heal ringworm symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Calendula officinalis teas can be used to treat ringworm, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and an increased risk of secondary infections if not properly cleaned and prepared. To use Calendula officinalis teas safely, take precautions such as testing a small area of skin before applying, avoiding eye contact, and keeping the affected area clean and dry to minimize the risk of complications.
preparation_usage: Wash hands before handling the infected area and Calendula tea
Soak a cotton ball with 5 tablespoons of Calendula tea
Apply the cotton ball to the infected area 3 times a day
Leave the cotton on for 30 minutes to an hour
Repeat for 7-10 days or until symptoms disappear

preparations.herb_desc: Cymbopogon citratus teas, derived from the lemongrass plant, have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, a fungal infection caused by Trichophyton species. The antifungal and antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation help to combat the infection by inhibiting the growth of fungal spores and preventing the spread of the disease. The bioactive constituents, including citral and geraniol, exhibit potent antifungal activity, helping to reduce the severity and duration of the infection. The benefits of Cymbopogon citratus teas in treating ringworm include reduced symptoms, accelerated healing, and prevention of complications, making it a valuable natural remedy for this common ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes. Then, strain the liquid.
Add 2 tablespoons of honey to the liquid for taste, if needed.
Let the tea cool down to a comfortable temperature for drinking.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, 2-3 times a week, for relief from ringworm symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cymbopogon citratus teas can be used to treat ringworm, but using them may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and increased heart rate in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Cymbopogon citratus teas, including using it in moderation, avoiding prolonged use, and being aware of potential interactions with other medications.
preparation_usage: Wash ringworm area with soap and water before treatment.
Drink 1 cup of Cymbopogon citratus tea 3 times a day.
Add 2 tablespoons of Cymbopogon citratus leaves to 1 cup of boiling water.
Soak affected area in warm Cymbopogon citratus tea bath for 30 minutes.
Continue treatment for 2-3 weeks or until ringworm disappears.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, including ringworm, due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents present in these teas, such as gingerols and shogaols, have been found to exhibit antifungal activity, helping to combat the fungal infections that cause ringworm. These compounds help to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making Zingiber officinale teas a potential natural remedy for ringworm. The benefits of using these teas to treat ringworm include reduced risk of antibiotic resistance, fewer side effects, and improved overall health.
preparation_procedure: Get 2 tablespoons of dried Zingiber officinale root.
Add the root to 1 cup of boiling water.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the root.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 3 times a day for 7-10 days.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Zingiber officinale teas showed antifungal activities against Candida albicans, a fungus that causes ringworm, and its growth inhibition was comparable to the antifungal nystatin.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be used to treat ringworm, but possible side effects include skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased risk of bleeding due to its anticoagulant properties. To minimize potential risks, it is recommended to start with small doses, avoid ingesting large amounts, and monitor your body's response for any adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Soak a cotton ball with 1 tablespoon of Zingiber officinale tea.
Apply the cotton ball to the ringworm area 3 times a day.
Use 1/2 cup of Zingiber officinale tea as a wash 2 times a day.
Drink 1 cup of Zingiber officinale tea 2 times a day.
Continue treatment for 2-3 weeks or until ringworm symptoms disappear.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, a fungal infection affecting the skin. The antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to combat the infection and reduce symptoms. The bioactive constituents, including curcumin and turmeric oil, exhibit potent antifungal activity and modulate the immune response to alleviate the condition. Regular consumption of Curcuma longa teas has been found to promote healing, reduce itching and inflammation, and prevent recurrence of the infection, offering a natural and effective remedy for ringworm.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa roots. You can buy it from a store or online.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Curcuma longa roots to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Then, strain the tea.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day for 2-3 weeks to help treat ringworm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the Journal of ethnopharmacology, Curcuma longa teas, specifically in the form of turmeric oil, have shown to be effective in treating ringworm, with significant improvement in lesions observed within 2-5 days and complete disappearance of lesions within 6-7 days after application.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating ringworm, however, it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, or an increased risk of bleeding in individuals with bleeding disorders, which can manifest as redness, itching, or swelling at the site of application. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to use Curcuma longa teas in moderation, avoid applying it to broken or sensitive skin, and monitor your body's response closely, especially if you are prone to allergic reactions or have a history of bleeding disorders.
preparation_usage: Use 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder per cup of water as a tea.
Boil the mixture for 5-7 minutes to release active compounds.
Drink 2 cups of Curcuma longa tea per day for 2-3 weeks.
Apply a paste made from 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa powder mixed with water to affected areas.
Repeat steps 3 and 4 for 2-3 weeks for optimal ringworm treatment.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe vera teas have been used as a natural remedy to treat the ringworm ailment due to its anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties, which help to soothe and heal the affected skin. The bioactive constituents of aloe vera, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and aloe-ependin, exhibit antifungal and antimicrobial activities that inhibit the growth of the ringworm fungus, Trichophyton. By consuming aloe vera teas, the body can absorb these beneficial compounds, which then help to reduce the severity of symptoms and promote the healing of the affected skin. The benefits of using aloe vera teas to treat ringworm include reduced inflammation, accelerated healing, and a decrease in the risk of infection, making it a popular natural remedy for this common skin condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of aloe vera gel, 1 cup of water, 1 tablespoon of honey (optional), and a tea strainer.
Combine aloe vera gel and water in a saucepan. Heat on low heat, stirring until the gel dissolves.
Remove from heat and let cool. Strain the mixture using a tea strainer to remove any lumps.
Add honey to taste, if using. Stir well to combine.
Drink 1/4 cup of the aloe vera tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe ringworm symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe vera teas can be used to treat ringworm, but using it may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, or allergic reactions in some individuals, particularly those with sensitive skin or allergies. To use Aloe vera teas safely, it is essential to start with a small dose, gradually increase the amount, and monitor for any signs of adverse reactions, such as excessive sweating, fever, or skin irritation, which may worsen the condition in some cases.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of aloe vera tea, 2-3 times a day
Take 1 tablespoon of aloe vera gel, 2 times a day
Apply aloe vera gel directly to affected area, 2-3 times a day
Continue treatment for 2-3 weeks, or until ringworm disappears
Monitor and adjust treatment as needed, with a doctor's guidance

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, a fungal infection caused by Trichophyton spp., due to its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid derivatives, help to inhibit the growth of fungal pathogens and reduce inflammation, thereby promoting healing and preventing further infection. The antifungal properties of Echinacea purpurea teas help to treat ringworm by suppressing the fungal growth and preventing its spread, while the anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with the infection. Overall, Echinacea purpurea teas offer a natural and effective way to treat ringworm, providing relief from symptoms and promoting a speedy recovery.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers.
Use 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over 1/4 cup of the Echinacea flowers in a cup.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture using a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea for taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for ringworm treatment, but consult a doctor first.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat the ringworm ailment, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, or an increased risk of liver damage in rare cases. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, individuals should avoid taking it in large quantities, be cautious when combining it with other medications, and monitor their skin for any signs of allergic reactions or worsening of symptoms.
preparation_usage: Boil 1 cup of water for Echinacea purpurea tea.
Steep 2-3 teaspoons of dried Echinacea purpurea in hot water.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea 2-3 times a day for 7-10 days.
Use Echinacea tea as a compress on affected skin for 15-20 minutes.
Repeat using Echinacea tea as a compress for 7-10 days in total.

preparations.herb_desc: Aloe barbadensis teas have been traditionally used to treat the ringworm ailment due to their anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Aloe barbadensis, such as aloin, aloe-emodin, and acemannan, help to inhibit the growth of fungi and bacteria that cause ringworm, thereby promoting healing and reducing inflammation. Drinking Aloe barbadensis teas helps to treat ringworm by providing internal support to the immune system and promoting the body's natural healing processes, while also helping to reduce symptoms such as itching and redness. The benefits of using Aloe barbadensis teas to treat ringworm include a natural and non-invasive approach that is free from harsh chemicals and side effects, making it a popular choice for those seeking a holistic remedy.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Aloe barbadensis leaves and 1 cup of water.
Wash the Aloe leaves with clean water to remove dirt.
Blend the Aloe leaves with 2 cups of water until smooth.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a cup.
Drink the Aloe tea 2-3 times a day for 1-2 weeks, and apply it directly to the affected area 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Aloe barbadensis teas can be effective in treating ringworm, but possible side effects may include skin irritation, allergic reactions, or digestive issues in some individuals, such as diarrhea or stomach cramps. Precautions should be taken when using Aloe barbadensis teas, including avoiding its use during pregnancy or breastfeeding, and not consuming it in excess, as it may cause dehydration.
preparation_usage: Wash the affected area with soap and water daily.
Steep 1 teaspoon of Aloe barbadensis in 1 cup of boiling water for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool to room temperature.
Soak a clean cloth in the tea and apply it to the affected area for 30 minutes, 2 times a day.
Drink 1/2 cup of the Aloe barbadensis tea 2 times a day for 2 weeks.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat the ringworm ailment due to their antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the fungal infections that cause this condition. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis, including carnosic acid, carnosol, and ursolic acid, possess strong antifungal and antioxidant activities that help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. These properties aid in treating the ringworm ailment by inhibiting the growth of the fungal pathogens, reducing the severity of symptoms, and promoting a faster recovery. The benefits of using Salvia officinalis teas to treat ringworm include reduced risk of complications, faster healing times, and a lower risk of recurrence.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the leaves in boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to the tea for taste.
Drink 1 cup of the tea, 2-3 times a day, for relief from ringworm symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be used as a potential treatment for ringworm, but using it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, digestive issues, and skin irritation in some individuals. It is recommended to take precautions when using Salvia officinalis teas to treat ringworm, including starting with small doses, monitoring your body's response, and being cautious of potential interactions with other medications or health conditions, especially if you have sensitive skin or allergies.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Salvia officinalis tea 3 times a day.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Salvia officinalis to 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Continue treatment for 2-3 weeks or until ringworm symptoms subside.
Monitor your condition and adjust dosage if needed (up to 2 cups a day).

preparations.herb_desc: Allium sativum teas, also known as garlic tea, have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, an infectious fungal infection. The antimicrobial properties of this herbal preparation, particularly its allicin and sulfur compounds, help to inhibit the growth of the fungus and reduce inflammation. The bioactive constituents, including quercetin and kaempferol, have been shown to possess antifungal and antiseptic properties, which aid in the treatment of ringworm. The benefits of using Allium sativum teas to treat ringworm include reduced symptoms, accelerated healing, and a natural alternative to conventional medications.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Allium sativum leaves and 2 cups of water.
Heat the water in a saucepan and bring it to a boil.
Add the fresh Allium sativum leaves to the boiling water and reduce heat.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain it using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink 1/4 cup of the tea, 3 times a day, for 7-10 days to help treat ringworm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "International journal of dermatology", Allium sativum teas for ringworm may be an effective treatment option, as the aqueous extract of garlic (A. sativum) inhibited 90% of dermatophyte isolates at a dilution of 1:100.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Allium sativum teas can be used to treat ringworm, but possible side effects include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Allium sativum teas to treat ringworm, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with a low dose, avoiding prolonged use, and monitoring for signs of adverse reactions, as excessive consumption may exacerbate the condition.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Allium sativum tea 3 times a day.
Take 2 tablespoons of Allium sativum powder 3 times a day.
Apply a paste made from Allium sativum to affected area.
Repeat application 2-3 times a day until symptoms improve.
Continue treatment for 2-4 weeks for best results.

preparations.herb_desc: Origanum vulgare teas have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, a fungal infection caused by Trichophyton species. The antifungal and antiseptic properties of this herbal preparation help to combat the infection by inhibiting the growth of fungal spores and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents, including carvacrol, thymol, and borneol, exhibit potent antifungal activity, which helps to alleviate symptoms and promote healing. Regular consumption of Origanum vulgare teas may also help to boost the immune system, reducing the risk of recurrence and promoting overall recovery from the ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Origanum vulgare leaves.
Steep 1 tablespoon of the leaves in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid into a cup and discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea to sweeten, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe ringworm symptoms.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Origanum vulgare teas can be used as a potential treatment for ringworm, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and digestive issues in some individuals, including stomach upset and diarrhea. Precautions should be taken when using Origanum vulgare teas to treat ringworm, such as avoiding its use in individuals with sensitive skin or allergies, and monitoring its effects to prevent overuse and potential interactions with other substances.
preparation_usage: Soak a cotton ball in Origanum vulgare tea, then apply to ringworm area.
Use 3 tablespoons of Origanum vulgare tea, 3 times a day.
Leave the cotton ball on the ringworm area for 30 minutes to an hour.
Replace cotton ball with a new one soaked in fresh Origanum vulgare tea.
Repeat steps 3-4 for 7-10 days, or until ringworm disappears.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, an infectious fungal infection, due to their antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of this herbal preparation, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, contribute to its therapeutic effects by inhibiting fungal growth and reducing inflammation. Lavandula angustifolia teas may help treat ringworm by reducing the severity of symptoms, promoting wound healing, and preventing the spread of the infection. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat ringworm include reduced risk of complications, faster recovery, and a natural alternative to conventional medications.

Steep the dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the mixture and let it cool.
Mix 2 tablespoons of the cooled Lavandula angustifolia tea with 1 cup of cool water.
Apply the cooled tea mixture to the affected area using a clean cloth 2-3 times a day.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat ringworm, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and gastrointestinal issues in some individuals. It is essential to take precautions when using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat ringworm, such as avoiding contact with the eyes, mouth, and sensitive areas, and starting with small doses to monitor for any adverse reactions.
preparation_usage: Measure 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep 1 teaspoon of Lavandula angustifolia tea in the water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain and let it cool to room temperature.
Apply the cooled tea to the affected area 3 times a day.
Repeat for 2-3 weeks or until the ringworm fades.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat the ringworm ailment due to their antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which help to combat the fungal infections that cause this condition. The bioactive constituents of Rosmarinus officinalis teas, including rosmarinic acid and camphor, exhibit potent antifungal activity, inhibiting the growth of fungi and promoting the healing of affected skin. By reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, Rosmarinus officinalis teas help to alleviate symptoms and speed up the recovery process, making them a valuable natural remedy for treating ringworm. The benefits of using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat ringworm include reduced risk of side effects, improved wound healing, and enhanced overall skin health.

Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the 2 tablespoons of dried Rosemary leaves to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea and let it cool before applying it to the affected area with a cotton ball.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be used to treat ringworm, but it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and stomach upset due to its high concentration of volatile oils. To use Rosmarinus officinalis teas safely, take precautions to avoid applying it to broken skin, and consider reducing the dosage if you experience any discomfort, and also use it in a well-ventilated area to minimize inhalation of its potent oils.

preparations.herb_desc: Solanum nigrum teas, derived from the leaves of the black nightshade plant, have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, a fungal infection of the skin. The anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce the severity of symptoms, such as itching and redness, and inhibit the growth of the fungus. The bioactive constituents, including solasonine, solamargine, and withanolides, exhibit potent antifungal activity, which contributes to the treatment of ringworm. The benefits of using Solanum nigrum teas to treat ringworm include reduced inflammation, accelerated healing, and a lower risk of recurrence.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Solanum nigrum leaves and 2 cups of water.
Crush the Solanum nigrum leaves to release their juice.
Boil the 2 cups of water in a pot, then add the crushed leaves.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, then strain it.
Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day to treat ringworm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Mycoses, Solanum nigrum teas for ringworm have been compared with conventional treatments in two clinical trials, with encouraging results reported.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Solanum nigrum teas can be used to treat ringworm, but it may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as nausea and diarrhea due to its high content of solanine, a toxic compound that can also cause vomiting and abdominal pain in some individuals. To use Solanum nigrum teas safely, one should be cautious of its potential allergenic effects, avoid taking large doses, and monitor their body's response closely, especially if they have sensitive skin or allergies.

preparations.herb_desc: Azadirachta indica teas have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, an infectious skin condition caused by a fungus. The antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to treat ringworm by inhibiting the growth of the fungus and reducing redness and itching. The bioactive constituents, including limonoids and terpenoids, present in Azadirachta indica teas have been found to exhibit potent antifungal activity, which is beneficial in treating ringworm. The benefits of using Azadirachta indica teas for treating ringworm include rapid healing, reduced risk of complications, and minimal side effects, making it a popular choice for natural remedies.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Azadirachta indica leaves and flowers. Clean and dry them.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot. Add 2 tablespoons of dried leaves and flowers.
Let the mixture boil for 5-7 minutes. Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup. Discard the solids. Let the tea cool.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 3 times a day. Repeat for 7-10 days. Consult a doctor if symptoms persist.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecules (Basel, Switzerland), Azadirachta indica teas have not been specifically mentioned for ringworm treatment in the provided study, but it mentions that Azadirachta indica has anti-inflammatory, anti-microbial, antioxidant, and wound recovery properties, which could be beneficial in managing atopic dermatitis, a condition that shares some similarities with ringworm.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Azadirachta indica teas can be effective in treating ringworm, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, skin irritation, and allergic reactions in rare cases, particularly when consumed in large quantities or without proper preparation. To use Azadirachta indica teas safely, it's essential to note that pregnant or breastfeeding women should avoid it, and individuals with sensitive skin or allergies should start with a small dose and monitor their reaction before increasing the amount.

preparations.herb_desc: Silybum marianum teas, also known as milk thistle tea, have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, an infectious skin condition caused by fungi. The anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties of Silybum marianum teas help to soothe and heal the affected area, reducing inflammation and promoting a healthy environment for recovery. The bioactive constituents of Silybum marianum teas, including flavonoids, saponins, and alkaloids, exhibit potent antifungal and anti-inflammatory activities that contribute to the treatment of ringworm. The benefits of using Silybum marianum teas to treat ringworm include reduced inflammation, accelerated healing, and the prevention of fungal infections, making it a promising natural remedy for this condition.
preparation_procedure: Gather Silybum marianum flowers and leaves. Use 1 cup of fresh or dried flowers and leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot. Remove from heat.
Add 1 cup of flowers and leaves to the water. Let it steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea and discard the flowers and leaves. Add honey to taste, if needed.
Drink 1-2 cups of the tea 2-3 times a day. Repeat for 2-3 weeks, or as directed by a healthcare professional.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Silybum marianum teas can be used to treat ringworm due to its antifungal properties, but possible side effects may include allergic reactions, skin irritation, and stomach upset in some individuals. When using Silybum marianum teas to treat ringworm, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with small doses, monitoring your body's response closely, and avoiding prolonged use to minimize the risk of adverse effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Matricaria chamomilla teas have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, an infectious skin condition caused by a fungus. The anti-inflammatory and antifungal properties of Matricaria chamomilla teas help to soothe the affected skin, reduce itching and inflammation, and prevent the spread of the infection. The bioactive constituents of Matricaria chamomilla, including flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, exhibit antifungal and antibacterial activities that contribute to its therapeutic effects. By using Matricaria chamomilla teas, individuals can benefit from its antiseptic and antipruritic properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms and promote healing of the affected area.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Matricaria chamomilla flowers.
Add 1 cup of boiling water to a cup.
Pour the boiling water over the dried flowers in a teapot.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain it.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help soothe ringworm symptoms.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Supportive care in cancer : official journal of the Multinational Association of Supportive Care in Cancer, Matricaria chamomilla teas showed promising results in reducing severity and lesion incidence with improved pain symptomatology.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Matricaria chamomilla teas can be used to treat ringworm, but using it may cause side effects such as skin irritation, allergic reactions, and increased sensitivity to sunlight. Precautions should be taken when using Matricaria chamomilla teas, such as starting with small amounts and monitoring for any adverse reactions, and avoiding its use if you have sensitive skin or are prone to allergic reactions.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat ringworm, a fungal skin infection caused by Trichophyton species. The antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties of yarrow tea help to treat this ailment by inhibiting the growth of fungal pathogens and reducing inflammation and redness in the affected area. The bioactive constituents of yarrow tea, including flavonoids and sesquiterpenes, contribute to its antifungal activity, making it an effective treatment option for ringworm. The benefits of using yarrow tea to treat ringworm include its non-toxic and natural approach, reduced risk of side effects, and ease of preparation and administration.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water.
Pour the boiling water over the dried Achillea millefolium in a cup.
Let the mixture steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the tea and drink 2-3 times a day to help treat ringworm.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Parasitology research, Achillea millefolium teas are not mentioned in the study for treating ringworm, as the study focuses on treatments for fleas and flies, as well as ear problems, but not ringworm specifically.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat ringworm, but it may cause side effects such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, or an increased risk of infection in people with weakened immune systems. When using Achillea millefolium teas to treat ringworm, it is essential to monitor your body's response closely and discontinue use if you experience any adverse reactions, and also take precautions such as applying a small patch test before using the tea on a larger area of skin.

preparation_study: According to the provided study, Melaleuca alternifolia teas have potential therapeutic effects against scabies, but there is no mention of its use specifically for ringworm, however, it is one of the medicinal plants reviewed for promising future treatments of skin diseases.
intro_study: According to "The Australasian journal of dermatology", teas for ringworm, such as tea tree oil, may reduce the symptomatology of the infection as effectively as traditional treatments but are no more effective in achieving a mycological cure.
